title: Compliance Status Trending

compliance.assurance_rationale: Monitors device configuration compliance status against Catalyst Center policies, providing evidence of configuration baseline enforcement.
compliance.controlObjective: Shows that compliant versus non-compliant device counts and compliance percentage are tracked over time (CM-6), not just at a point in time.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Report `UC-5.13.30` (daily timechart) stored in the compliance dashboard; optional email digest of the compliance_pct series for audit folders.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Demonstrates network device configuration compliance monitoring as part of firewall and router configuration standards.
compliance.controlObjective: Gives a running history that configuration standards (1.1.1) are not only defined but also monitored for sustained conformance.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same dashboard panel plus monthly CSV export of the timechart to evidence storage with retention that matches your PCI assessment cycle.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:compliance`. from the `compliance` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `complianceStatus` (string), `complianceType` (string), `deviceName` (string), `deviceUuid` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `lastUpdateTime` (epoch milliseconds). CIM data model: Change.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance" | timechart span=1d count(eval(complianceStatus="COMPLIANT")) as compliant count(eval(complianceStatus="NON_COMPLIANT")) as non_compliant | eval compliance_pct=round(compliant*100/(compliant+non_compliant),1)
description: Tracks compliance status over time to measure remediation progress, detect compliance drift, and demonstrate continuous compliance for auditors.
value: Auditors want to see continuous compliance, not point-in-time snapshots. Trending demonstrates that compliance is maintained and that violations are remediated promptly.
implementation: Enable the `compliance` input. Use a 30-90d window for the timechart. If divisor is zero, handle nulls in a wrapper query or add a guard eval for empty days.
visualization: Stacked area (compliant vs non_compliant), line chart of compliance_pct, annotations for change freezes.

references.title: Catalyst Center Compliance API ‚Äî Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Golden template update creating a step change in the compliance trend.** When the golden template is updated, the ratio of COMPLIANT to NON_COMPLIANT devices may shift dramatically on the day of the update. Distinguish by checking whether the trend shows a sharp, one-time shift corresponding to a template change. Suppress by annotating the trend chart with template change dates from audit logs.

**Compliance re-scan cadence affecting trend smoothness.** The compliance API polls hourly by default. Changes in compliance status may appear as step functions rather than smooth trends. Distinguish by checking the timechart span relative to the poll interval ‚Äî a `span=1d` with hourly polls should produce smooth trends, but `span=1h` may be noisy. No suppression needed ‚Äî adjust the timechart span to match the poll cadence.

**Device batch onboarding creating a temporary dip in the compliance ratio.** When many new devices are added to Catalyst Center, they may initially show as NON_COMPLIANT until their configurations are aligned to the golden template. Distinguish by checking whether the device count increased in the same period. Suppress by tracking new devices separately and allowing a 48-hour onboarding grace period.

**Seasonal compliance audit preparation changing compliance behavior.** Before a compliance audit, teams may run additional compliance checks or update templates, creating temporary turbulence in the trend. Distinguish by correlating with audit preparation schedules. No suppression needed ‚Äî this is expected pre-audit activity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ **compliance** modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (7538) writing `cisco:dnac:compliance` to `index=catalyst`.
‚Ä¢ Complete **UC-5.13.28** first so you have validated `complianceStatus` and `complianceType` in raw events; this report uses the same `GET /dna/intent/api/v2/compliance/detail` feed.
‚Ä¢ Default **30‚Äì90 day** time range for a credible trend; shorter windows are fine after a change freeze to prove recovery.
‚Ä¢ `docs/implementation-guide.md` for GRC export paths and `catalyst` index retention for audit evidence.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
‚Ä¢ **TA input** name: **compliance**; default poll **900 seconds**; assigned sourcetype `cisco:dnac:compliance`.
‚Ä¢ **Event shape:** the TA can emit **multiple** rows per device and per `complianceType` each poll. This SPL **counts event rows** per day, not ‚Äúunique devices.‚Äù For a true **per-device** daily score, pre-dedup in a subsearch or summary to **one row per device per day** (by policy family if you split dashboards).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the report
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance" | timechart span=1d count(eval(complianceStatus="COMPLIANT")) as compliant count(eval(complianceStatus="NON_COMPLIANT")) as non_compliant | eval compliance_pct=round(compliant*100/(compliant+non_compliant),1)
```

Understanding this SPL (ratio, empty days, policy scope)
‚Ä¢ **Guard** division by zero: when `compliant+non_compliant=0` for a day, append `| eval compliance_pct=if((compliant+non_compliant)=0, null(), ...)` (or a small conditional `eval`) so charts do not show a fake zero percent.
‚Ä¢ To trend **one** policy family, add a leading filter such as `complianceType="RUNNING_CONFIG"` to the base search‚Äîmixing **IMAGE** checks with **config** checks in one ratio can mislead executives without a shared denominator story.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
‚Ä¢ `timechart span=1d` counts how many **COMPLIANT** vs **NON_COMPLIANT** **evaluations** occurred in each calendar day (Splunk‚Äôs **span** uses the search time zone unless you set `tz`).
‚Ä¢ `compliance_pct` is the passing share of those two counted buckets for that day‚Äîdocument that **IN_PROGRESS** and **ERROR** are excluded unless you extend the `eval` list.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ Compare a given day‚Äôs **compliant** and **non_compliant** split to **Catalyst Center > Compliance** for the same **UTC** date; one-day skew often traces to **midnight** boundary or **double rows** per type.
‚Ä¢ Run `| timechart count` without splits to catch long **flat zero** days that really mean **ingest** or **scoping** problems, not ‚Äúperfect compliance.‚Äù
‚Ä¢ **Vendor UI parity:** cross-reference the Splunk results with the corresponding **Catalyst Center > Assurance** page for the same time window to confirm counts and values match.
Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize (evidence)
‚Ä¢ **Dashboard:** stacked area of `compliant` vs `non_compliant` with a line panel for `compliance_pct`; annotate known **CAB** or **upgrade** windows on the time axis for auditor narrative.
‚Ä¢ **Evidence:** schedule **weekly** CSV or PDF to your GRC store; the **NIST/PCI** references in this UC‚Äôs `compliance` array describe how assessors use the artefact‚Äîkeep the export path in your control matrix.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **Flat 100%** while operators see red in Catalyst: `complianceStatus` **case** or **props** change after a TA upgrade‚Äî`fieldsummary complianceStatus` and compare a raw event to the UI.
‚Ä¢ **Large step** in the line after a **device onboarding** wave: the **count** denominator jumped; split by `siteId` (if extracted) or add narrative to QBR slides.
‚Ä¢ If data is not arriving for `cisco:dnac:compliance`, check that the `compliance` input is enabled in the TA configuration and that the Catalyst Center API credentials have not expired.
grandmaExplanation: We draw a simple day-by-day picture of how many checks passed or failed, so you can show auditors that standards held steady or bounced back after a project‚Äînot just a one-day screenshot.
